task_completion.description: Verify that LibreCAD is open and the required layers are created with correct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD shows 'CONCRETE' (with a Gray or White color swatch) and 'HANDRAILS' (with a Red or Cyan color swatch).

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the concrete top landing and the first ramp run.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A square shape (representing the 60x60 top landing) connected to a long rectangular shape (representing the 48x360 first ramp run, which should be 7.5 times longer than it is wide), drawn in Gray or White.

task_completion.description: Verify the presence of the intermediate landing and the second ramp run, forming a U-shape with a gap.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A wide rectangle (representing the 60x108 intermediate landing) connecting the first ramp to a second long rectangle (48x360). The two long ramps must be parallel, forming a U-shape, with a visible narrow gap between them (representing the 12-inch gap, which should look about 1/4 the width of a ramp).

task_completion.description: Verify that continuous handrails are drawn on the correct layer and inset from the concrete edges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Red or Cyan lines (HANDRAILS layer) running parallel to the inside of the Gray/White concrete outlines. These lines must be visibly inset (representing the 2-inch gap) along all outer and inner edges of the ramps and landings, forming a continuous path.

task_completion.description: Verify that the drawing is saved to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar, a save dialog, or a terminal window shows the file saved as 'ada_ramp_plan.dxf' in the '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to draw the shapes rather than writing a script to generate the DXF file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the LibreCAD interface with drawing tools selected, command line inputs within the LibreCAD application, or active drawing crosshairs on the canvas.

integrity.description: The drawing consists of actual CAD entities rather than pasted images.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing canvas shows vector lines, selection handles (small squares on vertices when selected), or active CAD commands in the prompt area.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the LibreCAD interface and directly write the DXF file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows, if visible, do not contain python scripts, echo commands, or curl/wget commands that directly write or download the DXF file contents.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a U-shaped ADA ramp in LibreCAD. Verified specifications: 1) Layers: 'CONCRETE' (Gray/White) and 'HANDRAILS' (Red/Cyan). 2) Concrete dimensions: Top landing is 60x60, Ramp 1 is 48x360, Intermediate landing is 60x108, Ramp 2 is 48x360. 3) Gap: 12 inches between the two parallel ramp runs. 4) Handrails: Inset 2 inches from all concrete edges. 5) Save path: /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/ada_ramp_plan.dxf. Note: The VLM should judge dimensions based on relative proportions (e.g., ramps are 7.5x longer than they are wide, the gap is 1/4 the width of a ramp, and the handrail inset is very small compared to the ramp width).
